title: V-Field Magnitude Divergence from Observed Behavioral Baseline
narrative: Transactions display normal raw behavioral characteristics — amounts within expected range, standard timing patterns, consistent velocity, matching identity fields — yet Vesta engineered features register extreme values that contradict the clean surface presentation. V200, V258, and V307 register magnitudes in the 90th+ percentile while corresponding raw features remain unremarkable. This divergence suggests the transaction sequence contains hidden complexity invisible to human analysts: the model detects encoded relationships between device attributes, card metadata, and transaction context that fraudsters cannot replicate authentically. The V-field ensemble captures multi-dimensional interactions (device-card-amount-time combinations) that appear normal when examined individually but form statistically improbable patterns when evaluated as composite signatures. These engineered features often encode fraud-ring coordination signals where multiple operators use slightly varied parameters that individually pass validation but collectively form detectable clusters in high-dimensional feature space.
indicators: V200 >= 6 (p90 threshold), elevated sequence-pattern encoding despite normal behavioral metrics
V258 > p85 while card1_amt_zscore between -1.0 and +1.0, magnitude inconsistency
V307 in top decile while TransactionDT hour-of-day distribution matches cardholder baseline
V280 elevated above p80 with D1 > 5 indicating device-card pairing looks established
V305 > median + 2*IQR while C1-C14 card counters all within normal ranges
V320 magnitude exceeds p90 despite email domain and addr1 both matching prior transactions
indicator_explanations: Model's sequence-pattern feature flags unusual transaction ordering despite clean behavior
Engineered amount-context feature shows anomaly while actual amount appears normal
Time-based composite feature elevated despite transaction occurring during typical hours
Device-metadata interaction feature flags risk even though device appears established
Multi-feature interaction score anomalous while individual card-activity counters look fine
Identity-relationship encoding feature elevated despite email and billing matching history
summary: Transactions where Vesta engineered features register extreme values (p85-p90+) while all corresponding raw behavioral signals remain within normal ranges. The model detects complex multi-feature interactions invisible to direct observation.
reasoning: Fraudsters can replicate individual behavioral patterns but cannot authentically reproduce the high-dimensional feature interactions that Vesta engineering captures. When V-fields diverge from their expected values given clean raw features, it indicates synthetic transaction construction rather than genuine cardholder behavior.
